evals.prompt:
Review my resume based on the Toss Securities server developer JD.

JD highlights:
- Java/Kotlin, Spring Framework, Redis, Kafka
- High-volume traffic, concurrency handling, real-time data processing
- Data consistency and reliability without data loss
- Troubleshooting, performance tuning

The resume is in _config.yml.
evals.expected_output: Note load, Pre-eval research (JD + company), Self-intro type-based eval (A/B/C/D + global), C1-C5, career 6-criteria/Problem-Solving 6 criteria, depth-based problem-solving eval, JD keyword matching + content selection, Cross-section consistency check, AI Tone Audit, Phase 11 Quality Gate: resume-claim-examiner dispatch for ALL evaluator-eligible items + 2-3 alternatives + APPROVE/opt-out loop, Phase 12 output: HTML report + user approval → Phase 13 Note accumulate: HTML file with summary statistics (P0 N items, P1 N items, etc.), Section-by-section inline HTML comment blocks with CSS classes (.comment-p0, .comment-p1, etc.) and suggested revisions, bridge pattern check, generic opener detection, portfolio theme diversity, skill section placement

assertions.description: Phase 0: checks $OMT_DIR/review-resume/ and reports note status (loaded or auto-seeding)
Performs JD analysis: team identification, keyword extraction (technical/domain/culture), implicit problems from JD
Performs company research via WebSearch/WebFetch: core values, tech blog, product/service, career page — at least 2 channels attempted
Research results visibly influence which Type A/B/D aspects are highlighted in evaluation
Each self-intro paragraph identified by type (A/B/C/D) and evaluated against type-specific criteria
Global criteria (paragraph count, independence, first sentence, original framing) evaluated after per-type
No S1-S5 codes anywhere in output
When suggesting Type C content, the suggested opening is a capability/experience claim — NOT a philosophy/belief statement
Type C recommendation references company research findings
After findings, presents 2-3 concrete options with trade-offs
Type D not failed for lacking metrics
Does not parrot JD keywords back in self-intro suggestions
Distinguishes weak from strong matches in JD keyword analysis
Career bullets evaluated with career-specific criteria (causal linkage, metric specificity, role clarity, differentiation, interview hook potential, section fit) in natural language
Problem-solving entries evaluated with PS-specific criteria (exploratory causation, reasoning depth, cognitive attribution, alternative comparison, interview depth, section fit) in natural language
Career bullets evaluated for interview hook quality — does this line provoke interviewer curiosity?
Problem-solving entries evaluated for interview depth — can this entry survive 3-level pushback?
Career uses linear causation (goal→action→outcome) while problem-solving uses diagnostic causation (detection→diagnosis→attempt→failure→solution)
Career evaluates outcome differentiation (beyond industry standard) while problem-solving evaluates reasoning depth (alternative comparison + trade-off analysis)

Career and problem-solving sections are evaluated independently with separate section-specific criteria. Note: cross-section consistency between self-introduction and other sections IS checked separately.
Performs Developer Competency Assessment with C1-C5 axes, rating each as STRONG/PRESENT/WEAK/ABSENT with cited evidence
C1-C5 assessment specifies career level and applies career-level expectations
For WEAK/ABSENT axes that are EXPECTED/REQUIRED, provides specific gap recommendations
Phase 8: classifies problem-solving entries by depth (signature/detailed/compressed) before evaluation
Applies depth-appropriate criteria: P.A.R.R. for signature, P1+P2+P5 for detailed, volume guide for compressed

Evaluation output demonstrates knowledge from reference files — e.g., uses P.A.R.R. dimensions (P1-P5/P6-P8), Type A-D criteria, career/PS evaluation criteria, or depth classification flowchart. Evidence: specific dimension codes or criteria language that only exists in references/.
Phase 10 explicitly addresses AI Tone Audit — either invokes Skill(humanizer) or declares intent to invoke it (e.g., '[Would invoke Skill(humanizer)]'). The phase must not be skipped.

Phase 11: resume-claim-examiner agent is dispatched for ALL evaluator-eligible items (career bullets, problem-solving entries, self-intro with technical content) — either actual dispatch or declaration of intent. Eligibility is by content type, not P-level findings.

Phase 11: generates 2-3 revision alternatives with tradeoff comparison (safe/high-impact/balanced directions) for each evaluator-eligible item, based on pre-examiner interview results, before resume-claim-examiner dispatch

Phase 11: each evaluator-eligible item requires resume-claim-examiner APPROVE or explicit user opt-out before proceeding to Phase 12. No item advances without one of these two outcomes. Mandatory Verdict Tracker blocks Phase 12 entry if any eligible item lacks a verdict.

Phase 11: conducts pre-examiner interview with user for each evaluator-eligible item — presents findings, collects context, discusses alternatives, reaches agreement BEFORE dispatching to resume-claim-examiner
Phase 13: proposes note accumulation (new candidates, updates, preferences) and asks for user confirmation
Phase 12 HTML output includes a summary statistics section showing P0-P3 severity counts (e.g., P0 N items, P1 N items) and total finding count
Phase 12 HTML output uses div blocks with CSS classes (comment-p0, comment-p1, comment-p2, comment-p3) to display inline feedback per finding — not markdown blockquotes
Each finding is classified with a priority level: P0 (must fix), P1 (should fix), P2 (nice to have), or P3 (optional)
Each finding includes a concrete suggested revision — not just diagnosis but also a rewritten version
When JD is provided, actively recommends which career bullets, problem-solving entries, or self-intro types to feature/swap based on JD relevance
Checks that projects/episodes mentioned in self-introduction have corresponding entries in problem-solving or career sections. Flags hooks without backing evidence.
Type A evaluation considers bridge pattern: bold opener → reason(bridge) → episode 3-beat structure
Evaluation checks for generic openers (differentiation failure: the 10-person test) and self-claim endings (e.g., 'I am the type who...'). Flags if found, passes if resume has no such patterns.
Type C closing evaluated for contribution framing — flags desire expressions ('I want to...') and recommends capability/contribution vision instead

When [Problem] section is verbose (2+ signals: biz+tech mixed, 5+ lines, 3+ lines before core issue), recommends [Overview]+[Problem] split. When not verbose, stating 'split unnecessary' or simply not mentioning it counts as PASS.
Treats retrospective as optional; flags sentimental growth narrative as anti-pattern but accepts architectural limit + extension direction (2-3 lines)
Checks theme diversity across problem-solving entries; flags when 2+ entries share the same theme (consistency, performance, stability, business impact, etc.)
Evaluates technical skill section placement — checks if skill section is positioned for JD keyword matching within 7-second recruiter scan

Phase tracking markers use [Phase N/13: ...] format with correct total (13), and phase names in markers match SKILL.md Phase Map: 0=Note Load, 1=Pre-Evaluation Research, 2=Self-Introduction Evaluation, 3=Target Position Gate, 4=Developer Competency Assessment, 5=Section-Specific Evaluation, 6=3-Level Pushback Simulation, 7=First-Page Primacy + JD Keyword Matching, 8=Problem-Solving Evaluation, 9=Technical Substance Verification, 10=AI Tone Audit, 11=Per-Bullet Content Quality Gate, 12=Generate HTML Report, 13=Note Accumulate

evals.prompt:
Review only the problem-solving section of my resume. I'm applying to Daangn's payments team. It's in _config.yml.

JD highlights:
- Payment system design/operation
- Stability in high-volume traffic environments
- Incident response and resilience
- Kotlin, Spring Boot
evals.expected_output: Note load (with candidate pool check for problem-solving/), depth classification of all entries, depth-specific evaluation, JD keyword matching + content selection, candidate pool recommendation if available, Phase 11 Quality Gate: resume-claim-examiner dispatch for ALL evaluator-eligible entries + 2-3 alternatives + APPROVE/opt-out loop, Phase 12 output: HTML report + user approval → Phase 13 Note accumulate: HTML file with summary statistics (P0 N items, P1 N items, etc.), Section-by-section inline HTML comment blocks with CSS classes (.comment-p0, .comment-p1, etc.) and suggested revisions, overview/problem split awareness, portfolio theme diversity

Classifies each problem-solving entry by depth (signature/detailed/compressed)
Signature depth entries get comprehensive evaluation covering all P.A.R.R. dimensions (P1-P5) — problem detection, failure arc, why-chain, stopping judgment, reflection
Either evaluates compressed entries with volume guide, OR detects their absence and recommends adding compressed entries per career-level guide
Evaluation criteria match the candidate's career level (not applying junior criteria to mid/senior or vice versa)
Recommends ordering problem-solving entries by JD relevance (payments, incident response prioritized)
If note has problem-solving candidates, mentions available alternatives or suggests swaps for JD fit
Proposes accumulating new insights from this review session
Uses depth classification flowchart, P.A.R.R. dimensions (P1-P5/P6-P8), or career-level criteria from references/problem-solving.md

Each finding includes a concrete suggested revision — not just diagnosis but also a rewritten version
When JD is provided, actively recommends which career bullets, problem-solving entries, or self-intro types to feature/swap based on JD relevance

When [Problem] section is verbose (2+ signals: biz+tech mixed, 5+ lines, 3+ lines before core issue), recommends [Overview]+[Problem] split. When not verbose, stating 'split unnecessary' or simply not mentioning it counts as PASS.
Treats retrospective as optional; flags sentimental growth narrative but accepts architectural limit/extension direction
Checks theme diversity across problem-solving entries; flags 2+ entries sharing same theme (consistency, performance, stability, business impact, etc.)

Phase 11: resume-claim-examiner agent is dispatched for ALL evaluator-eligible items (career bullets, problem-solving entries, self-intro with technical content) — either actual dispatch or declaration of intent. Eligibility is by content type, not P-level findings.

Phase 11: generates 2-3 revision alternatives with tradeoff comparison (safe/high-impact/balanced directions) for each evaluator-eligible item, based on pre-examiner interview results, before resume-claim-examiner dispatch

Phase 11: each evaluator-eligible item requires resume-claim-examiner APPROVE or explicit user opt-out before proceeding to Phase 12. No item advances without one of these two outcomes. Mandatory Verdict Tracker blocks Phase 12 entry if any eligible item lacks a verdict.

evals.prompt: Review my resume. I'm a new grad developer with only one internship experience. It's in _config.yml.
evals.expected_output: Note load, Pre-eval research (no target company → no company research), Self-intro type-based eval (Type A/B/D; multiple FAIL expected due to thin experience), Phase 2 interview trigger (experience-mining.md Phase 2: self-introduction ANY type FAIL), C1-C5 competency assessment with WEAK/ABSENT on C2/C3/C4 axes, Phase 4 interview trigger (experience-mining.md Phase 4: ANY axis WEAK/ABSENT), Section-specific evaluation, Phase 5 interview trigger (ANY criterion FAIL), 4-Stage Bypass Protocol executed per triggered phase (direct → reframing → adjacent → routine), Discovered Candidates Working Set populated with mined experiences, graceful opt-out if user declines interview, Phase 11 Quality Gate: resume-claim-examiner dispatch for ALL evaluator-eligible items + 2-3 alternatives + APPROVE/opt-out loop, Phase 12 HTML report + user approval → Phase 13 Note accumulate

assertions.description: Phase 0: checks note directory and reports status (self-introduction/career/problem-solving candidate counts)
Does NOT perform company research when no target company is specified
Each self-intro paragraph identified by type (A/B/D) and evaluated against type-specific criteria
Global criteria evaluated after per-type (paragraph count, independence, first sentence, original framing)
No S1-S5 codes anywhere in output
Phase 2/4/5 interview trigger activated due to WEAK/ABSENT/FAIL conditions — agent invokes experience-mining.md protocol for at least one phase

4-Stage Bypass Protocol executed per triggered phase: Stage 1 (Direct Question) → Stage 2 (Bypass/Reframing) → Stage 3 (Adjacent Experience) → Stage 4 (Daily Work). Each stage only entered if previous stage yielded no source.
Discovered Candidates Working Set records at least one mined experience in format: Phase {N} | {topic} | Fact: {fact} | Context: {context} | Verifiable: {metric} | Target: {section}
If user says 'next' or 'move on', agent exits current phase interview gracefully, falls back to static Writing Guidance, and continues to next phase — does not force interview continuation

Performs Developer Competency Assessment with C1-C5 axes, rating each as STRONG/PRESENT/WEAK/ABSENT/N/A with cited evidence; C3-C5 axes may be rated N/A when not expected at the candidate's career level
C1-C5 assessment specifies career level (New Grad/Junior) and applies career-level expectations
For WEAK/ABSENT axes that are EXPECTED/REQUIRED at junior level, provides specific gap recommendations or triggers Phase 4 interview
Phase 8: classifies problem-solving entries by depth (signature/detailed/compressed) before evaluation
Applies depth-appropriate criteria per entry type
Evaluation output demonstrates knowledge from reference files — P.A.R.R. dimensions, Type A-D criteria, career/PS evaluation criteria, or depth classification language
Phase 10 addresses AI Tone Audit — invokes or declares intent to invoke Skill(humanizer)
Phase 13: proposes note accumulation (new candidates, updates, preferences) and asks for user confirmation
Phase 12 HTML output includes a summary statistics section showing P0-P3 severity counts (e.g., P0 N items, P1 N items) and total finding count
Phase 12 HTML output uses div blocks with CSS classes (comment-p0, comment-p1, comment-p2, comment-p3) to display inline feedback per finding — not markdown blockquotes
Each finding is classified with a priority level: P0 (must fix), P1 (should fix), P2 (nice to have), or P3 (optional)
Each finding includes a concrete suggested revision — not just diagnosis but also a rewritten version

Phase 11: resume-claim-examiner agent is dispatched for ALL evaluator-eligible items (career bullets, problem-solving entries, self-intro with technical content) — either actual dispatch or declaration of intent. Eligibility is by content type, not P-level findings.

Phase 11: generates 2-3 revision alternatives with tradeoff comparison (safe/high-impact/balanced directions) for each evaluator-eligible item, based on pre-examiner interview results, before resume-claim-examiner dispatch

Phase 11: each evaluator-eligible item requires resume-claim-examiner APPROVE or explicit user opt-out before proceeding to Phase 12. No item advances without one of these two outcomes. Mandatory Verdict Tracker blocks Phase 12 entry if any eligible item lacks a verdict.

Phase 11: conducts pre-examiner interview with user for each evaluator-eligible item — presents findings, collects context, discusses alternatives, reaches agreement BEFORE dispatching to resume-claim-examiner

benchmark_summary.notes: 127 assertions across 4 evals (46+35+21+25), 88.0% pass. branch-check removed (moved to resume-apply), anti-pattern redefined (generic opener + self-claim endings), verbose signal criteria added, bold-topic guidance removed, Phase 8 depth count output added. Eval 3: 100% pass. Eval 4 added: experience mining trigger + 4-stage bypass + opt-out verification. Phase 11 Quality Gate assertions added (3 x 2 evals = 6 new). Eval 3/4 quality-gate assertions added (3 x 2 evals = 6 new).

benchmark_history.notes: s3-s4-na and no-company-specific-content assertions were design issues

benchmark_history.changes: Pre-eval research expansion, Type C capability-first, A/B/D Reason JD context, Type C domain diversification
Developer Competency Assessment (C1-C5) added as Phase 4, all phases renumbered (9→10)
Persistent Memory System (Phase 0/10), Problem-Solving unified framework (depth: signature/detailed/compressed), SKILL.md split into 375-line core + 6 reference files, Korean→English conversion

Assertion improvements: reads-reference-files→applies-reference-criteria (check content not Read() calls), ai-tone-audit→ai-tone-audit-phase (accept declared intent), compressed-volume-guide→compressed-depth-handling (accept absence detection), reads-problem-solving-reference→applies-problem-solving-criteria
Phase 10 output format (Summary Table + Inline Feedback + Cherry-pick), Phase tracking internal-only, JD-based content selection, Cross-Section Consistency Rule, assertion overhaul
Memory system simplification: ~/.omt/resume-manage/ → $OMT_DIR/ dynamic binding, frontmatter tags-only (depth/rating/used_in/variant removed), accumulation rules 6→4, resume-apply path fix

3-reviewer feedback: Memory→Note rename (6 assertions), new assertions added (Eval1: +8, Eval2: +7, Eval3: +4 = 19 new). Reference file updates: bridge pattern, assertive opener anti-pattern, Type C closing, overview/problem split, bold topic format, retrospective guidance, portfolio theme diversity, skill section placement

D-code system fully removed (D1c-D6c/D1p-D6p → natural language criteria: causal linkage, metric specificity, etc). Mandatory Evaluation Checklists added to self-introduction.md, problem-solving.md, section-evaluation.md. D-code assertions → substance-based (career-differentiated-evaluation, ps-differentiated-evaluation, etc). Pass rate 74.2% → 81.4% (+7.2%)

branch-check removed (moved to resume-apply), anti-pattern redefined (generic opener + self-claim endings), verbose signal criteria added, bold-topic guidance removed, Phase 8 depth count output added. 97→92 assertions. Eval 3: 100% pass.
